enrichment.short_description: Ulysses is a distraction-free Markdown writing app for Mac, iPad, and iPhone with project organization and direct publishing.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Ulysses is a Mac, iPad, and iPhone writing app with Markdown editing, project organization, and direct blog publishing. From $5.99/month.

features.title: Markdown-based Editor
features.description: A distraction-free writing environment that uses simple text markup for formatting.

features.title: Project Organization
features.description: Organize large writing projects with a library, groups, and filters.

features.title: Grammar & Style Checker
features.description: A built-in checker supports more than 20 languages.

features.title: Multi-format Export
features.description: Export finished work to PDF, Word, ebook formats, and more.

features.title: Direct Publishing
features.description: Publish directly to WordPress, Ghost, Medium, and Micro.blog.

features.title: Cross-device Sync
features.description: iCloud sync keeps writing up to date across Mac, iPad, and iPhone.

features.title: Writing Goals
features.description: Set word-count goals and track progress toward them.

pros: Clean, distraction-free writing interface
Deep project and library organization for long-form work
Native apps for Mac, iPad, and iPhone with iCloud sync
Built-in grammar and style checking in 20+ languages
Direct publishing integrations to WordPress, Ghost, Medium, and Micro.blog
cons: No Windows or web version
No permanent free plan — a paid subscription is required after the trial
Markup-based formatting has a learning curve for new users

faqs.question: What platforms does Ulysses run on?
faqs.answer: Mac, iPad, and iPhone; there is no Windows or web version.

faqs.question: Is there a free plan?
faqs.answer: No permanent free plan; Ulysses offers a free trial before requiring a paid subscription.

faqs.question: How much does Ulysses cost?
faqs.answer: $5.99 per month, or $39.99 per year billed annually, per the official pricing page.

faqs.question: Can I publish directly from Ulysses?
faqs.answer: Yes, to WordPress, Ghost, Medium, and Micro.blog.

faqs.question: Does Ulysses support grammar checking?
faqs.answer: Yes, its built-in checker covers more than 20 languages.

use_cases.title: Long-form authors
use_cases.description: Writers drafting books or long manuscripts use Ulysses' library and project organization to manage multiple documents.

use_cases.title: Bloggers
use_cases.description: Bloggers write in Ulysses and publish directly to WordPress, Ghost, or Medium without leaving the app.

use_cases.title: Academic and professional writers
use_cases.description: Writers producing structured documents use goals and export options to move from draft to a finished PDF or Word file.

features: Access on Mac, iPad, and iPhone
Apple Family Sharing for up to 5 members
Same features as the monthly plan, billed yearly

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Ulysses?
paragraphs: Ulysses is a writing application built around a distraction-free, Markdown-based editor combined with project management tools for large documents. It runs natively on Mac, iPad, and iPhone, syncing writing across devices via iCloud.

The app organizes work with a library and project structure, includes a grammar and style checker supporting more than 20 languages, and lets writers export or publish directly to formats like PDF, Word, and ebooks, or to platforms such as WordPress, Ghost, Medium, and Micro.blog.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Ulysses is for
paragraphs: Ulysses suits authors, bloggers, and professional writers on Apple devices who want a focused writing environment with strong document organization, without needing Windows or web access.

enrichment.short_description: Obsidian is a free, local-first Markdown note app with backlinks, graph view, canvas, and an open plugin API.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Obsidian is a free Markdown note-taking app with backlinks, graph view, canvas, and a plugin API. Optional paid Sync and Publish add-ons.

features.title: Local Markdown Files
features.description: Notes are stored as plain-text Markdown files you control.

features.title: Bidirectional Linking
features.description: Link notes together and see backlinks for every note.

features.title: Graph View
features.description: Visualize the relationships between notes.

features.title: Canvas
features.description: A visual space for brainstorming and diagramming.

features.title: Plugin Ecosystem
features.description: Thousands of community plugins and themes, built on an open API.

features.title: Optional Sync & Publish
features.description: Paid add-ons for cross-device sync and web publishing.

pros: Core app is completely free with no feature limits
Notes stored as local plain-text Markdown files, avoiding lock-in
Extensive community plugin and theme ecosystem
Open API available for custom plugin development
Optional end-to-end encrypted Sync
cons: Sync and Publish require separate paid subscriptions
Steeper learning curve than simpler note apps
Organizational/commercial use requires a paid Commercial License

faqs.question: Is Obsidian free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the core app is free with no sign-up required; paid add-ons cover Sync and Publish.

faqs.question: Where are my notes stored?
faqs.answer: As local plain-text Markdown files.

faqs.question: Can I extend Obsidian?
faqs.answer: Yes, via an open API and a large community plugin ecosystem.

faqs.question: How much does Sync cost?
faqs.answer: $4/user/month billed annually, or $5/user/month billed monthly.

faqs.question: Is there a commercial license?
faqs.answer: Yes, $50/user/year for organizational use.

use_cases.title: Personal knowledge management
use_cases.description: Individuals build a personal wiki of linked notes stored locally as Markdown files.

use_cases.title: Developers extending Obsidian
use_cases.description: Developers build custom plugins and themes using Obsidian's open API.

use_cases.title: Teams needing shared vaults
use_cases.description: Teams pay for Sync to collaborate on shared vaults across devices.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Obsidian?
paragraphs: Obsidian is a note-taking application that stores notes as local plain-text Markdown files and links them together with backlinks and a graph view. It includes a Canvas feature for visual brainstorming and diagramming.

The core app is free with no sign-up required and no feature limits. Obsidian's open API supports an extensive community ecosystem of plugins and themes, and optional paid Sync and Publish services add cross-device synchronization and web publishing.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Obsidian is for
paragraphs: Obsidian fits people who want full ownership of their notes as local files, developers who want to extend the app via plugins, and teams willing to pay for Sync to collaborate on shared vaults.

enrichment.short_description: Capacities organizes notes as connected objects (people, books, projects) instead of files, with a free core plan and a paid Pro tier.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Capacities organizes notes as connected objects like people, books, and projects. Free core plan; Pro adds AI, queries, and calendar sync.

features.title: Object-based Notes
features.description: Treats entries as typed objects, such as people, books, or ideas, instead of plain files.

features.title: Daily Notes Inbox
features.description: Capture thoughts in a daily inbox before organizing them.

features.title: Backlinks
features.description: See all connections to a note at a glance.

features.title: Related Content
features.description: Automatic discovery of previously made connections.

features.title: Custom Object Types
features.description: Define your own structured object types.

features.title: Full Data Export
features.description: Export all content in standard formats on any plan.

features.title: AI Features (Pro)
features.description: AI chat, auto-fill properties, and smart queries on the Pro plan.

pros: Free plan with full data export and no credit card required
Object-based model goes beyond simple file/folder note apps
Automatic related-content discovery surfaces existing connections
GDPR-compliant, EU-based data centers
User-funded (Believer plan) rather than venture-capital backed
cons: Exact Pro and Believer prices are not published as fixed figures on the main pricing page
AI features are gated behind the paid Pro tier
No confirmed offline-first local file storage

faqs.question: Is Capacities free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the core product is free with full export capability and no credit card required.

faqs.question: What does Capacities Pro add?
faqs.answer: AI chat, smart queries, calendar integration, and task actions with tools like Todoist.

faqs.question: What is Capacities Believer?
faqs.answer: A higher-tier subscription positioned as supporting the company's mission.

faqs.question: Can I export my data?
faqs.answer: Yes, Capacities offers full data export in standard formats on every plan.

faqs.question: Is Capacities venture-backed?
faqs.answer: No, the company describes itself as user-funded rather than VC-backed.

use_cases.title: Personal knowledge management
use_cases.description: Individuals capture daily notes and connect them into an object-based knowledge base.

use_cases.title: Researchers organizing sources
use_cases.description: Researchers use custom object types to track papers, people, and projects as linked entities.

pricing_plans.price: Not publicly listed (monthly or yearly billing, ~16% annual savings)

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Capacities?
paragraphs: Capacities is a note-taking platform that organizes information as interconnected objects, such as people, books, or projects, rather than files in folders. A daily notes inbox lets users capture ideas before sorting them into structured object types.

The core product is free, with full data export and no credit card required. Capacities Pro adds AI chat, smart queries, and calendar integration, while a Believer tier supports the company's development.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Capacities is for
paragraphs: Capacities suits knowledge workers and researchers who want to organize information as structured, linked objects rather than a traditional folder hierarchy.

enrichment.short_description: Heptabase is a visual whiteboard-based knowledge base with an AI tutor and PDF annotation for students and researchers.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Heptabase is a visual whiteboard knowledge app with an AI tutor, PDF annotation, and Readwise/Zotero integration. Plans start at $8.99/month.

features.title: Visual Whiteboards
features.description: Card-based whiteboards for organizing notes and research.

features.title: PDF Annotation
features.description: Highlight and annotate PDFs directly in the app.

features.title: AI Tutor
features.description: AI-guided learning sessions, included on the Premium plan.

features.title: Bi-directional Linking
features.description: Connect notes and cards across whiteboards.

features.title: Web Clipper
features.description: Capture web content into the knowledge base.

features.title: Readwise & Zotero Integration
features.description: Import highlights and references from Readwise and Zotero.

features.title: Real-time Collaboration
features.description: Work with others on shared whiteboards.

features.title: Cross-platform Availability
features.description: Available on macOS, Windows, Linux, iOS, Android, and the web.

pros: Available on macOS, Windows, Linux, iOS, Android, and the web
Visual whiteboard approach suited to research and learning workflows
Built-in PDF annotation with Readwise and Zotero import
AI tutor and AI chat included on paid plans
Backed by Y Combinator, Kleiner Perkins, and other investors
cons: No permanent free plan; only a 7-day free trial
AI features are metered by monthly credit limits
Premium/Premium+ tiers are required for unlimited PDF parsing and top-tier AI models

faqs.question: Does Heptabase have a free plan?
faqs.answer: No permanent free plan is offered; a 7-day free trial is available.

faqs.question: How much does Heptabase cost?
faqs.answer: Pro is $8.99/month, Premium is $17.99/month, and Premium+ is $53.99/month (each cheaper billed yearly).

faqs.question: What AI models does Heptabase support?
faqs.answer: The Premium plan adds access to premium AI models from OpenAI, Google, and Anthropic.

faqs.question: Can I annotate PDFs?
faqs.answer: Yes, and Premium plans add unlimited PDF parsing and OCR.

faqs.question: What platforms is Heptabase available on?
faqs.answer: macOS, Windows, Linux, iOS, Android, and the web.

use_cases.title: Students building a knowledge base
use_cases.description: Students use whiteboards and the AI tutor to organize course material into a lasting knowledge base.

use_cases.title: Researchers managing literature
use_cases.description: Researchers import Zotero references and Readwise highlights onto visual whiteboards.

features: Unlimited PDF uploads and OCR parsing

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Heptabase?
paragraphs: Heptabase is a visual knowledge base built around card-based whiteboards, aimed at students, researchers, and lifelong learners. It combines note-taking, PDF annotation, and an AI tutor for guided learning sessions.

The app is available on macOS, Windows, Linux, iOS, Android, and the web, and integrates with Readwise and Zotero for importing highlights and references. Paid plans range from Pro at $8.99/month up to Premium+ at $53.99/month, each with a different monthly AI credit allowance.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Heptabase is for
paragraphs: Heptabase is aimed at students and researchers who want a visual, whiteboard-driven way to organize course material, papers, and notes, with AI assistance for learning and review.

enrichment.short_description: Milanote is a visual tool for organizing creative projects with notes, images, and boards, offering a free plan and paid Pro/Team tiers.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Milanote helps creative teams organize projects with visual boards, notes, and images. Free plan available; Pro plans start at $9.99/month.

features.title: Visual Boards
features.description: Combine notes, images, and files on a flexible visual board.

features.title: Multi-format Content
features.description: Save notes, images, video, and sketches from multiple sources.

features.title: Industry Templates
features.description: Templates for filmmaking, writing, design, photography, marketing, and more.

features.title: Team Collaboration
features.description: Share boards with edit, comment, or view-only permissions.

pros: Free plan available with no time limit
Used by well-known companies including Nike, IDEO, Netflix, and Google
Wide range of industry-specific templates
Flexible collaboration permissions (edit/comment/view)
Available on desktop, web, and mobile (iOS/Android)
cons: Free plan is capped at 100 notes/images/links and 10 file uploads
Team plan pricing rises for larger groups (up to 50 people at $49/month billed annually)
Monthly billing ($12.50) costs more than annual billing ($9.99/month)

faqs.question: Is Milanote free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan allows up to 100 notes, images, or links and 10 file uploads with no time limit.

faqs.question: How much is Milanote Pro?
faqs.answer: $9.99/month billed annually, or $12.50/month billed monthly.

faqs.question: Is there a Team plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, $49/month billed annually for 10-50 people; larger teams should contact sales.

faqs.question: What platforms does Milanote support?
faqs.answer: Desktop, web, and mobile apps for iOS and Android, plus iPad.

faqs.question: Does Milanote offer templates?
faqs.answer: Yes, for filmmaking, writing, design, photography, marketing, and other creative fields.

use_cases.title: Creative project planning
use_cases.description: Creative teams collect inspiration and organize projects visually on shared boards.

use_cases.title: Content and marketing planning
use_cases.description: Marketers and content teams use templates to plan campaigns and content visually.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Milanote?
paragraphs: Milanote is a visual organization tool for creative projects that lets users combine notes, images, video, and sketches on flexible boards. It includes industry-specific templates for filmmaking, writing, design, photography, and marketing.

The Free plan supports up to 100 notes, images, or links with 10 file uploads and unlimited shared boards. Pro removes those limits starting at $9.99/month billed annually, and a Team plan covers larger groups.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Milanote is for
paragraphs: Milanote is built for creative teams and individuals, such as designers, filmmakers, and marketers, who need a visual way to collect inspiration and plan projects.

enrichment.short_description: Scrintal is a visual note-taking and research tool built on an infinite canvas, with an AI assistant, for Mac, Windows, and web.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Scrintal is a visual research tool with an infinite canvas, AI assistant, and templates for organizing notes, PDFs, and connected ideas.

features.title: Infinite Canvas
features.description: A freeform space for brainstorming and organizing content.

features.title: Multi-format Content
features.description: Add PDFs, images, notes, videos, and documents to boards.

features.title: Bi-directional Links
features.description: Visually connect ideas with arrows and links.

features.title: AI Assistant
features.description: Automates repetitive organization tasks.

features.title: Templates
features.description: Templates built for study and research workflows.

features.title: Real-time Collaboration
features.description: Work together on boards live.

features.title: Docs from Blocks
features.description: Turn canvas blocks into documents with a click.

pros: Infinite canvas supports flexible visual organization
Native apps for Mac (Apple Silicon and Intel) and Windows, plus web access
AI assistant helps automate repetitive organizational tasks
Stated user base of 10,000+ learners and researchers
Includes study and research templates
cons: Exact subscription pricing is not displayed as plain text on the official site
No confirmed free plan
Smaller company (Scrintal Labs AB) compared to larger note-taking competitors

faqs.question: What is Scrintal?
faqs.answer: A visual note-taking and research tool built around an infinite canvas.

faqs.question: What platforms does Scrintal support?
faqs.answer: macOS (Apple Silicon and Intel), Windows, and the web.

faqs.question: Does Scrintal include AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, an AI Assistant is built in to help automate repetitive tasks.

faqs.question: Is there a paid plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Scrintal Pro offers unlimited docs/boards, unlimited file uploads, real-time collaboration, and priority support, billed monthly or annually.

faqs.question: Where is Scrintal based?
faqs.answer: Scrintal Labs AB is headquartered in Stockholm, Sweden.

use_cases.title: Visual research organization
use_cases.description: Researchers connect PDFs, notes, and ideas on an infinite canvas instead of linear documents.

use_cases.title: Study planning
use_cases.description: Students use Scrintal's study templates and canvas to organize course material.

pricing_plans.price: Not publicly listed (billed monthly or annually)

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Scrintal?
paragraphs: Scrintal is a visual note-taking and research platform organized around an infinite canvas, where users add PDFs, images, notes, videos, and documents and connect them with visual links.

An AI Assistant automates repetitive organizational tasks, and study/research templates help structure boards. Scrintal is available on macOS, Windows, and the web, from Scrintal Labs AB in Stockholm, Sweden.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Scrintal is for
paragraphs: Scrintal is aimed at researchers, academics, and students who prefer organizing notes and sources visually on a canvas rather than in linear documents.

enrichment.short_description: Plane is an AI-native project management platform with cycles, a wiki, and dashboards, with a free cloud tier and self-hosted deployment.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Plane is an AI-native project management tool with cycles, wikis, and dashboards. Free for up to 12 users; Pro starts at $6/seat/month.

features.title: Multiple Views
features.description: Board, Spreadsheet, List, and Gantt views for work items.

features.title: Cycles
features.description: Time-boxed cycles with velocity tracking and burndown charts.

features.title: Built-in Wiki
features.description: Project documentation alongside tasks.

features.title: Dashboards
features.description: Real-time tracking of project metrics.

features.title: Initiatives & Epics
features.description: Rollup tracking across multiple projects.

features.title: Plane AI
features.description: Context-aware AI agents for task automation.

features.title: Migration Tools
features.description: Import from Jira, Linear, ClickUp, Asana, and Monday.com.

features.title: Self-hosted Option
features.description: Docker or Kubernetes deployment with full data ownership.

pros: Free cloud tier for up to 12 users with 500 AI credits/seat
Self-hosted deployment available for full data ownership
Migration tools from major competitors (Jira, Linear, ClickUp, Asana, Monday.com)
Built-in wiki, dashboards, and initiative/epic tracking
SOC 2, ISO 27001, GDPR, and CCPA compliance noted
cons: Advanced features (custom work item types, time tracking) require the paid Pro tier
Enterprise Grid requires a custom quote
AI features are metered by per-seat credit allowances

faqs.question: Is Plane free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan is $0/seat/month for up to 12 users with core project management features.

faqs.question: How much is Plane Pro?
faqs.answer: $6/seat/month, with a discount for annual billing.

faqs.question: Can Plane be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, commercial and air-gapped self-hosted editions are available alongside the cloud product.

faqs.question: Does Plane include AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, "Plane AI" offers context-aware agents, with AI credits allotted per plan.

faqs.question: Can I migrate from other tools?
faqs.answer: Yes, Plane supports migration from Jira, Linear, ClickUp, Asana, and Monday.com.

use_cases.title: Startup project tracking
use_cases.description: Small teams use the free tier's boards, cycles, and dashboards to manage projects.

use_cases.title: Enterprise program management
use_cases.description: Larger organizations use Business or Enterprise Grid plans for advanced workflows, approvals, and audit logs.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted deployments
use_cases.description: Organizations needing full data ownership deploy Plane via Docker or Kubernetes.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Plane?
paragraphs: Plane is an AI-native project management platform combining task tracking, documentation, and AI-powered workflows in one workspace. It offers Board, Spreadsheet, List, and Gantt views, time-boxed cycles, a built-in wiki, and real-time dashboards.

Plane is available as a free cloud tier for up to 12 users, paid Pro and Business cloud tiers, a custom-quoted Enterprise Grid plan, and self-hosted commercial or air-gapped editions with migration tools from Jira, Linear, ClickUp, Asana, and Monday.com.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Plane is for
paragraphs: Plane fits startups on its free tier, growing teams needing advanced workflow and dashboard features on Pro or Business, and enterprises or security-conscious organizations that need self-hosted or air-gapped deployment.

enrichment.short_description: OpenProject is open-source project management software with Gantt charts and agile boards, offering a free Community edition and paid tiers.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: OpenProject is open-source project management software with Gantt charts and agile boards. Free Community edition; paid plans from €5.95/user/month.

features.title: Gantt Charts
features.description: Project planning and scheduling with Gantt charts.

features.title: Agile Boards
features.description: Scrum and Kanban boards for agile teams.

features.title: Team Planner
features.description: Visualize workload across the team.

features.title: Time Tracking & Cost Reporting
features.description: Track time and costs against projects.

features.title: Meeting Management
features.description: Plan and document team meetings.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Connects with Nextcloud, GitHub, GitLab, OneDrive/SharePoint, and XWiki.

features.title: API & Customization
features.description: An open API for integration and customization.

pros: Community Edition is free, open source (GPL v3), with no minimum user count
Available as self-hosted or Enterprise Cloud SaaS
Wide integration support, including GitHub, GitLab, Nextcloud, and OneDrive/SharePoint
Notable users include Siemens, Deutsche Bahn, and the Linux Foundation
14-day free trial for cloud and on-premises paid tiers
cons: Paid tiers require a minimum of 25 users (Basic/Professional) or 100+ (Premium)
Monthly billing carries a €1/user surcharge on Cloud Basic
Corporate tier requires a custom quote and 250+ users

faqs.question: Is OpenProject free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Community Edition is free and open source under GPL v3.

faqs.question: How much do paid plans cost?
faqs.answer: Basic is €5.95/user/month, Professional €10.95, and Premium €15.95 (25+ or 100+ user minimums apply); Corporate is custom-priced.

faqs.question: Can I try a paid plan first?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day trial is available for cloud and on-premises versions.

faqs.question: What languages does OpenProject support?
faqs.answer: English, German, Spanish, French, and Portuguese, among others.

faqs.question: Can OpenProject integrate with other tools?
faqs.answer: Yes, including Nextcloud, GitHub, GitLab, OneDrive/SharePoint, and XWiki.

use_cases.title: Open-source self-hosting
use_cases.description: Organizations self-host the free Community Edition for full control over their data.

use_cases.title: Enterprise project portfolios
use_cases.description: Larger organizations use paid tiers for professional support, security add-ons, and Enterprise Cloud hosting.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is OpenProject?
paragraphs: OpenProject is an open-source project management platform offering Gantt charts, agile Scrum and Kanban boards, a Team Planner, time tracking, and cost reporting. It is licensed under GPL v3 and can be self-hosted or used via Enterprise Cloud.

Paid tiers (Basic, Professional, Premium, Corporate) add enterprise features and support levels, with a 14-day free trial available for cloud and on-premises versions. It integrates with tools including Nextcloud, GitHub, GitLab, and OneDrive/SharePoint.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who OpenProject is for
paragraphs: OpenProject suits organizations that want an open-source, self-hostable project management tool, as well as enterprises that need professional support and Enterprise Cloud hosting.

enrichment.short_description: Taiga is an open-source agile project management tool with Kanban and Scrum boards, offering a free cloud tier and self-hosted deployment.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Taiga is open-source agile project management software with Scrum and Kanban boards. Free cloud tier available; paid plans from €5/month.

features.title: Kanban Boards
features.description: Customizable boards with swimlanes and WIP limits.

features.title: Scrum Module
features.description: Sprint planning, backlog management, and burndown charts.

features.title: Issue Tracking
features.description: Customizable issue types and priorities.

features.title: Dashboards & Reporting
features.description: Team performance metrics and audit trails.

features.title: Custom Roles & Fields
features.description: Configurable roles, permissions, stages, and custom fields.

features.title: Zapier Integration
features.description: Connect Taiga to other tools via Zapier.

pros: Open source with a free cloud tier (1 public + 1 private project)
Self-hosted and Private Cloud deployment options available
Both Scrum and Kanban methodologies supported natively
Translated into more than 20 languages
Recognized as a high performer on G2 and Capterra
cons: Free tier limited to 10MB storage and one public/private project
Higher tiers (Basic, Premium) needed for meaningful storage and unlimited projects
Private Cloud and On-Premise pricing require a custom quote

faqs.question: Is Taiga free?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free cloud tier exists with 10MB storage and one public and one private project.

faqs.question: How much does Taiga cost?
faqs.answer: Enthusiast is €5/month, Basic €20/month, and Premium €60/month (cheaper billed annually); Private Cloud and On-Premise require a custom quote.

faqs.question: Can Taiga be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, On-Premise deployment is available with priority support and SLA options.

faqs.question: Does Taiga support Scrum and Kanban?
faqs.answer: Yes, both methodologies are built in as separate modules/board types.

faqs.question: How many languages does Taiga support?
faqs.answer: It has been translated into more than 20 languages.

use_cases.title: Agile software teams
use_cases.description: Cross-functional teams run sprints and track backlogs using Taiga's Scrum module.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted deployments
use_cases.description: Organizations requiring on-premise control deploy Taiga behind their own firewall or VPN.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Taiga?
paragraphs: Taiga is a free, open-source agile project management platform offering Kanban boards, a Scrum module with sprint planning and burndown charts, and customizable issue tracking.

It is available as a free cloud tier, paid cloud tiers (Enthusiast, Basic, Premium) with increasing storage and support levels, and custom-quoted Private Cloud or On-Premise deployment for teams that need to self-host.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Taiga is for
paragraphs: Taiga suits agile software teams running Scrum or Kanban workflows, as well as organizations that prefer self-hosted or private-cloud deployment for security or compliance reasons.

enrichment.short_description: Leantime is an open-source project management tool designed for neurodivergent users, with a free self-hosted edition and paid cloud plans.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Leantime is open-source project management built for ADHD, dyslexia, and autism. Free self-hosted edition; cloud Pro from $8/user/month.

features.title: My Work Dashboard
features.description: A personal workspace with time blocking and notes.

features.title: Multiple Project Views
features.description: Kanban, Table, and List views alongside Gantt charts.

features.title: AI Task Prioritization
features.description: AI-powered suggestions to help prioritize tasks.

features.title: Retrospectives & Idea Management
features.description: Built-in team reflection and idea-tracking tools.

features.title: Timesheets & Time Tracking
features.description: Track hours against projects.

features.title: Goal-based Structure
features.description: Link tasks to organizational goals.

features.title: Whiteboards & Blueprints
features.description: Visual planning tools for projects.

pros: Free, self-hosted open-source Community Edition (AGPLv3)
Designed specifically for neurodivergent users, including people with ADHD, dyslexia, and autism
14-day free trial on the paid cloud plan, no credit card required
Same feature set on monthly and annual paid plans
AI-powered task breakdown and status report generation
cons: No free cloud/SaaS tier — the free option requires self-hosting
AI features are limited by a monthly credit allowance (500 credits) on Pro
Enterprise features (SSO, audit logs, data residency) require custom pricing

faqs.question: Is Leantime free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the open-source Community Edition is free to self-host under AGPLv3.

faqs.question: How much is the cloud plan?
faqs.answer: $10/user/month billed monthly, or $8/user/month billed annually.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: Who is Leantime designed for?
faqs.answer: It's designed for neurodivergent users, including people with ADHD, dyslexia, and autism.

faqs.question: Does Leantime include AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, including AI-powered task prioritization and status report generation, with 500 AI credits/month on Pro.

use_cases.title: Neurodivergent-friendly project management
use_cases.description: Individuals and teams who find traditional PM tools overwhelming use Leantime's simplified, goal-based workflow.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted teams
use_cases.description: Organizations needing full control deploy the free open-source Community Edition via Docker.

features: Same features as the monthly plan

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Leantime?
paragraphs: Leantime is an open-source project management platform positioned as "project management for the non-project manager," designed specifically for neurodivergent users such as people with ADHD, dyslexia, and autism.

It offers a personal My Work Dashboard, Kanban/Table/List views, Gantt charts, whiteboards, timesheets, and AI-powered task prioritization. The Community Edition is free and open source (AGPLv3) for self-hosting, while a hosted cloud Pro plan starts at $8/user/month billed annually, with a 14-day free trial.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Leantime is for
paragraphs: Leantime is aimed at individuals and teams, including neurodivergent professionals, who want a simplified, goal-based project management workflow, whether self-hosted for free or via the paid cloud plan.
